content.problem_latex:
Find the smallest whole number between $$14$$ and $$40$$ that is divisible by $$3$$ and by $$4$$.
(A) 12
(B) 16
(C) 18
(D) 24
(E) 36
content.problem_plain: Find the smallest whole number between 14 and 40 that is divisible by 3 and by 4 . (A) 12 (B) 16 (C) 18 (D) 24 (E) 36
content.solution_latex: Multiple of $$3$$ and $$4$$ also a multiple of $$12$$.  $$12, 24, 36, 48, \cdots $$  Smallest multiple between $$14$$ and $$40$$ is $$24$$.
content.solution_plain: Multiple of 3 and 4 also a multiple of 12 . 12, 24, 36, 48, Smallest multiple between 14 and 40 is 24 .

step_outline: Multiple of $$3$$ and $$4$$ also a multiple of $$12$$.

search.problem_text: Find the smallest whole number between 14 and 40 that is divisible by 3 and by 4 . (A) 12 (B) 16 (C) 18 (D) 24 (E) 36
search.solution_text: Multiple of 3 and 4 also a multiple of 12 . 12, 24, 36, 48, Smallest multiple between 14 and 40 is 24 .

content.problem_latex:
The values of length and width of a rectangle are both prime numbers. If the area of that rectangle is $10$, what is its perimeter?
(A) 5
(B) 7
(C) 10
(D) 14
content.problem_plain: The values of length and width of a rectangle are both prime numbers. If the area of that rectangle is 10 , what is its perimeter? (A) 5 (B) 7 (C) 10 (D) 14
content.solution_latex: $10=2\times 5$,  the length is $5$ and the width is $2$.  $(2+5)\times 2=14$.
content.solution_plain: 10=2 5 , the length is 5 and the width is 2 . (2+5) 2=14 .

step_outline: $10=2\times 5$,  the length is $5$ and the width is $2$.

search.problem_text: The values of length and width of a rectangle are both prime numbers. If the area of that rectangle is 10 , what is its perimeter? (A) 5 (B) 7 (C) 10 (D) 14
search.solution_text: 10=2 5 , the length is 5 and the width is 2 . (2+5) 2=14 .

content.problem_latex:
The product of two whole numbers is $$5$$. What is the sum of these two numbers?
(A) 10
(B) 6
(C) 5
(D) 4
(E) None of the above
content.problem_plain: The product of two whole numbers is 5 . What is the sum of these two numbers? (A) 10 (B) 6 (C) 5 (D) 4 (E) None of the above
content.solution_latex: If the product of two whole numbers is $$5$$, then one of the numbers is $$5$$ and the other is $$1$$. Their sum is $$6$$.
content.solution_plain: If the product of two whole numbers is 5 , then one of the numbers is 5 and the other is 1 . Their sum is 6 .

step_outline: If the product of two whole numbers is $$5$$, then one of the numbers is $$5$$ and the other is $$1$$.

content.problem_latex:
Which of the following cases can\textquotesingle t make an even number?
(A) An odd number$$+$$An even number
(B) An even number$$+$$An even number
(C) An odd number$$+$$An odd number
content.problem_plain: Which of the following cases can t make an even number? (A) An odd number + An even number (B) An even number + An even number (C) An odd number + An odd number
content.solution_latex: The sum of an odd number and an even number is always an odd~ number. The sum of two numbers which are both even or both odd is always an even number.
content.solution_plain: The sum of an odd number and an even number is always an odd~ number. The sum of two numbers which are both even or both odd is always an even number.

step_outline: The sum of an odd number and an even number is always an odd~ number.
The sum of two numbers which are both even or both odd is always an even number.

search.problem_text: Which of the following cases can t make an even number? (A) An odd number + An even number (B) An even number + An even number (C) An odd number + An odd number
search.solution_text: The sum of an odd number and an even number is always an odd~ number. The sum of two numbers which are both even or both odd is always an even number.

content.problem_latex:
An unknown number is an odd number greater than $$50$$ but less than $$100$$. The number is a multiple of $$3$$ and $$7$$. What is the value of the unknown number?
(A) 63
(B) 77
(C) 84
(D) 91
(E) None of the above
content.problem_plain: An unknown number is an odd number greater than 50 but less than 100 . The number is a multiple of 3 and 7 . What is the value of the unknown number? (A) 63 (B) 77 (C) 84 (D) 91 (E) None of the above
content.solution_latex: A multiple of $$3$$ and $$7$$ also a multiple of $$21$$  $$21\times3=63$$  $$21\times4=even$$  $$21\times5=105$$, exceed  Thus, only $$63$$
content.solution_plain: A multiple of 3 and 7 also a multiple of 21 21 3=63 21 4=even 21 5=105 , exceed Thus, only 63

step_outline: A multiple of $$3$$ and $$7$$ also a multiple of $$21$$  $$21\times3=63$$  $$21\times4=even$$  $$21\times5=105$$, exceed  Thus, only $$63$$
